channel.title: Stakeholder Analysis on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Stakeholder Analysis on RaihnForge

item.title: Lark Resorts Software Solutions Plan
item.description:
Lark Resorts is a fictional client built for the IT 260 final project, a Colorado-based hospitality company maintaining two properties in Vail and Telluride and preparing to expand into Alaska and California. The assignment was to write an executive summary that the company’s leadership could plausibly hand to a board, recommending a standardized software stack to support that expansion. What follows is that document.

The Mission

We must reconstruct our technology processes and applications in order to facilitate a cohesive, robust, responsible, and efficient infrastructure allowing for future growth and sustainment in our next organizational evolution.

item.title: COVID Uplift: Team Project Charter
item.description:
Editor’s note: classmate names in the original document have been replaced with Teammate 1, Teammate 2, and Teammate 3 in keeping with this archive’s privacy conventions.

This is the kickoff artifact for the ADMG 374 team project. Four students worked entirely over Microsoft Teams and Zoom across the COVID winter quarter to deliver a hypothetical nonprofit website serving Kittitas County. I was the named Project Manager on this charter. The charter bundles the project charter proper, the business case, a stakeholder analysis, and the team contract, all signed off at the front of the quarter.
